old_claim.note: Water Carrying Techniques provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No water-carrying dependency remains because using liquid in a recipe does not establish water transport as a distinct enabling technology, and the soap source does not document that relationship.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract: discusses the written record and material chemistry of ancient soap production but does not identify water-container transport as an enabling process.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source establishes deliberate soap production without a separate water-carrying technology claim.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Ingredient availability and transport technology are different graph concepts; ordinary water use does not demonstrate the latter.

ontology_before: Any process using water inherited a generic water-carrying edge regardless of how water was obtained or used.
ontology_after: The soap node is limited to the evidenced chemical production record rather than unrelated transport infrastructure.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: soap_making_basic no longer directly depends on water_carrying_techniques.
would_reject_if: A source showed that transporting water in specialized containers was a necessary step in the earliest documented soap process.
short_reason: Water as an ingredient is not evidence for water-carrying technology.
